expectations.text: Output explicitly characterizes the data regime for AM (continuous temporal stream from in-situ sensors, limited labeled examples, expensive evaluations per print)
expectations.evidence: Abstract states 'temporal and spatial structure is fundamentally non-i.i.d.'; bibliography section notes 'label-sparse for microstructure: melt pool geometry is directly measurable; grain structure requires destructive characterization' and 'label scarcity' explicitly; data regime section characterizes scan-track temporal autocorrelation, non-stationarity, and label expense

expectations.text: Output flags that the sequential/temporal nature of thermal history data violates the i.i.d. assumption of standard feedforward models, and names which method families handle this
expectations.evidence: 'The i.i.d. assumption fails completely at the layer level: treating each layer's thermal map as an independent observation ignores the causal thermal inheritance'; LSTM/GRU/Transformer encoder family explicitly named as handling this; 'i.i.d.-assuming MLPs' listed under 'Incompatible ML families'

expectations.text: Sim-to-real gap is named with at least one specific physics the simulation omits (e.g., melt pool dynamics, microstructure evolution, residual stress)
expectations.evidence: Abstract: 'primary gap sources are unmodeled melt pool fluid dynamics (Marangoni convection, recoil pressure), emissivity uncertainty in pyrometry, and process-geometry coupling not captured by layer-level thermal finite element models'; conclusion section also names these explicitly

expectations.text: An engineering baseline is identified — what a process engineer would use without ML (e.g., PID control, design of experiments, empirical process maps)
expectations.evidence: Abstract: 'Engineering baselines — layer-averaged pyrometry thresholds and fixed PID power controllers — remain the only broadly hardware-validated references in most comparative studies'; dedicated section on engineering baselines with quantified metrics (PID achieves ~10% reduction in melt pool area variance)

expectations.text: References use numbered format [1], [2] inline with a numbered list at the end
expectations.evidence: Inline citations [1, 2], [3], [4] used throughout; full numbered reference list starting with [1] Shevchik et al. (2018)

expectations.text: Method selection for at least one family is explicitly justified from problem characteristics (data regime, uncertainty need, or physics structure) — not just described
expectations.evidence: 'The fundamental argument for recurrent architectures in AM thermal process control is not preference — it follows from the physics. Thermal state at layer N is causally inherited from layers 1 to N-1 through heat conduction; the functional relationship between prior states and current state is precisely what LSTM, GRU, and Transformer encoder architectures model.'
